review_title: obfuscation of JavaScript frontend and backend
description: confident that our intellectual property is protected as good as possible
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: state-of-the-art JavaScript obfuscation features for frontend & backend software and constantly new obfuscation features are getting added in order to keep up with reverse engineering threats
cons: rarely it happens that obfuscation introduces bugs in the frontend software

review_title: Industrial strength obfuscation, protection and support
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Jscrambler has deep knowledge of Javascript protection to protect intellectual property and prevent tampering. They have many features and options. Their web console is easy to use, informative and powerful. They also provide HTTP APIs that allow us to programmatically use their service and integrate with our builds. Jscrambler support is top notch.
cons: It is not cheap and it is usage based so if you have many builds and environments, you must watch your usage.

description: Would not recommend this software to anyone.
author: Eli -.
pros: The documentation was reasonably comprehensive, aside from performance benchmarks.
cons: Outdated product with low engineering investment. There are two popular kinds of protection: legacy JS obfuscation (what Jscrambler is known for, not very useful these days) and VM obfuscation. Their legacy obfuscation is not much different than other free and open source packages, and does not offer much protection. They have also attempted to offer modern VM obfuscation, but this is poorly tested and has terrible performance that makes it unusable in many applications. Unfortunately they don't let you try this before buying it.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Perfect solution for securing your intellectual property
description: Love the product. Does exactly what we need and the support is great. Keep it up!
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: High levels of protection, including date and domain locks.
cons: There have been features that stopped being supported or work differently on newer releases, requiring we redesign our our interface to the Jscrambler API.

description: It's a good product. The support it's incredible, the overall result of using this tool is too. The only complains are little things that can easily be implemented in no time
author: Gines O.
pros: - It has a good integration with webpack - The support it's incredible fast and they solve the problems in no time - The resulting code it's non-sense and the performance doesn't drop that much - You can trick the configuration in order to fit your necessities
cons: - There's a leak of options that other obfuscators have like prevent variable content to be obfuscated or make the code isolated and self-executed - In the past there were a few bugs that cause some problems but the technical team fixed them fast

description: The product/service is the best in the market and the Customer support is second-to-none.
author: Jay W.
pros: Ease of use. Strength of obfuscation. Minimal impact to performance.
cons: There were no cons associated with Jscrambler.

description: This is convenient tool for obfuscation. I checked couple of tools on web for this, but this could be best one. Price is not cheap a little bit.
author: Chanhyoung L.
pros: Javascript obfuscation. A lot of options for obfuscation.
cons: I have to re-create templates whenever this software is updated.

review_title: A must have for web-based solutions
description: Great to improve web-based product' security and protect IP.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: JScrambler has been a great help for our web-based products since it helps obfuscating our javascript code, which is good for security reasons and protect our intellectual property.
cons: I believe that there's a good margin to improve the learning curve when starting to use the product. Not that hard to use, but it could improve.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Gabriele P.
pros: JScrambler is easy to setup and customize, even in a custom setup. It does what it promises and we especially appreciated the webpack integration.
cons: The license fee is not for all pockets/industries. We hit on some minor bugs when targeting some older versions of JS
